summary.intent: Get PR #705 (branch codex/issue-277-marquee-core) through Codex automated review by resolving all review threads and achieving green CI in the fogodev/ars-ui repository.
summary.outcome: Two fixes were committed: the first (76e80200) corrected SyncProps interaction-pause state computation; the second added a loop-exhaustion guard to hover and focus resume paths, with a regression test and matching spec update. Both were pushed; the session ended before the second Codex review cycle completed.

repo: Codex review state is signaled via GitHub issue reactions: 👀 means actively reviewing, reaction drop means review complete (may open new threads). Poll reactions before checking threads.

The repo enforces spec/code parity: spec snippets in spec/components/data-display/marquee.md must be updated in lockstep with logic changes in the Rust source, enforced by `cargo xtask spec compile-snippets` and `cargo xtask spec validate`.
The effective merge gate is Codex approval plus green CI, not just green CI alone — the workflow explicitly waits for the Codex reaction to drop before considering review done.

PR review comments use threaded replies via GitHub API `in_reply_to` field; threads must be explicitly resolved via GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation. A new `@codex review` trigger is posted as an issue comment to restart the review cycle.

code.finding: SyncProps computes remaining enabled interaction-pause causes before selecting the next state; the bug was that it did not check this set before transitioning, causing a single disable to resume playback even when other causes remained.

Hover and focus-out resume paths lacked an exhaustion guard: if loop_count was shrunk to an already-exhausted value while paused, resuming via interaction-end would incorrectly restart playback instead of keeping the exhausted-paused state.
Test suite location for Marquee; the regression test `marquee_interaction_end_keeps_loop_exhausted_after_loop_count_shrink` was added here to cover the loop-exhaustion-during-pause edge case.

Spec sections covering interaction pause and finite-loop exhaustion behavior must mirror the Rust state machine exactly; the finite_loop_exhausted predicate and its interaction with resume paths are specified here.

workflow: Polling Codex review status requires two separate API calls per interval: reactions endpoint for 👀 presence, then GraphQL for unresolved thread list — neither alone is sufficient.

The focused test run (`cargo test -p ars-components --lib -- data_display::marquee`) plus `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy`, and both xtask spec commands form the minimum local gate before committing a Marquee fix.
Write the regression test first (red), then apply the fix (green) — the transcript explicitly follows this sequence to validate the fix is causally correct.

friction: Codex review latency required multiple 2-minute polling sleeps with no early-exit mechanism, making the wait-loop wall-clock time unpredictable and long.
No push notification or webhook available for Codex reaction changes; had to busy-poll via sleep intervals rather than event-driven wakeup.
The P2 thread (loop-exhaustion resume bug) was only surfaced after the first full review cycle completed, requiring a second full commit-push-review cycle.
open_items: Second Codex review cycle had not completed at session end — the exhaustion-guard commit still needs Codex approval and all CI lanes to go green.
Several CI lanes (coverage, release verification, unit tests, adapters, feature-flag) were still pending when the session ended; their results are unknown.
The interaction-pause/exhaustion interaction may have additional untested edge cases (e.g., loop_count shrunk while hover and focus are both active simultaneously).
